summary: Iteration 2 testing of new CelebTrack features (Videos/Shorts split, AI recommendations via Claude, Google News, Secondary channels, Trending context). Backend: 10/10 pytest tests passed against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL covering all new endpoints. Frontend: visually verified 4 main tabs (Videos/Shorts/Noticias/Funas manuales), 3 sub-tabs (Más recientes/Más virales del canal/Recomendados Facebook), 9:16 Shorts grid, Canales adicionales section with '+ Agregar canal', and Recomendados Facebook panel showing 'Editar contexto' + 'Generar recomendaciones' buttons with persisted trending_context (TEST_CTX) from backend PUT visible in UI.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to ~692 lines; consider splitting into routers (videos, recommendations, news, secondary_channels) for maintainability
AI recommendations endpoint blocks for 5-15s; consider returning a job id + polling or SSE for better UX on long Claude calls
/news endpoint cached 1h server-side - good; ensure cache key includes celeb_id+lang
Secondary channel POST payload key is 'youtube_channel_id' (correct, matches main channel) - documented for next agents
seed_data_creation: Test added/removed TEST secondary channel on Franco; set trending_context to 'TEST_CTX: temas virales tiktok mexico enero 2026' on Franco (still set, may want main agent to clear)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 10 new-feature backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_celebtrack_v2.py pass. POST /secondary-channels requires field 'youtube_channel_id' (not 'channel_id'). AI recommendations endpoint returns {recommendations:[{video_id,score,reason}], strategy:'...'} via Claude Sonnet 4.5 (~10s). News endpoint returns {news:[...]} from Google News RSS. UI structure verified via screenshots - all tabs/sub-tabs/sections render correctly. Generar-recomendaciones button click in UI was not exercised due to a script lambda bug, but backend AI flow is verified independently and Editar-contexto dialog opens (textarea is in DOM).
